title: chfn: PAM: System error
body:
**Description**

I was running buildah as root to work around fuse-overlayfs being really slow (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/2970).

However, when running as root with these commands with this Dockerfile:

```
FROM adoptopenjdk:11.0.7_10-jdk-hotspot
RUN addgroup --gecos '' foo \
  && adduser --disabled-password --gecos '' --ingroup foo foo
```

The workaround given in https://github.com/moby/moby/issues/6345 (from 2014) of adding `ln -s -f /bin/true /usr/bin/chfn` to the `RUN` command works, but I wouldn't expect this to be an issue in 2021 with buildah.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. Create Dockerfile shown above
2. Run: `sudo buildah bud --layers -t foo .`

**Describe the results you received:**

```
STEP 1: FROM adoptopenjdk:11.0.7_10-jdk-hotspot
STEP 2: RUN addgroup --gecos '' foo   && adduser --disabled-password --gecos '' --ingroup foo foo
Adding group `foo' (GID 1000) ...
Done.
Adding user `foo' ...
Adding new user `foo' (1000) with group `foo' ...
Creating home directory `/home/foo' ...
Copying files from `/etc/skel' ...
chfn: PAM: System error
adduser: `/usr/bin/chfn -f  foo' returned error code 1. Exiting.
error building at STEP "RUN addgroup --gecos '' foo   && adduser --disabled-password --gecos '' --ingroup foo foo": error while running runtime: exit status 1
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

I would expect build to work.

**Output of `rpm -q buildah` or `apt list buildah`:**

```
buildah-1.18.0-1.fc32.x86_64
```

**Output of `buildah version`:**

```
Version:         1.18.0
Go Version:      go1.14.10
Image Spec:      1.0.1-dev
Runtime Spec:    1.0.2-dev
CNI Spec:        0.4.0
libcni Version:  
image Version:   5.8.0
Git Commit:      
Built:           Wed Dec 31 19:00:00 1969
OS/Arch:         linux/amd64
```

**Output of `cat /etc/*release`:**

```
Fedora release 32 (Thirty Two)
NAME=Fedora
VERSION="32 (Thirty Two)"
ID=fedora
VERSION_ID=32
VERSION_CODENAME=""
PLATFORM_ID="platform:f32"
PRETTY_NAME="Fedora 32 (Thirty Two)"
ANSI_COLOR="0;34"
LOGO=fedora-logo-icon
CPE_NAME="cpe:/o:fedoraproject:fedora:32"
HOME_URL="https://fedoraproject.org/"
DOCUMENTATION_URL="https://docs.fedoraproject.org/en-US/fedora/f32/system-administrators-guide/"
SUPPORT_URL="https://fedoraproject.org/wiki/Communicating_and_getting_help"
BUG_REPORT_URL="https://bugzilla.redhat.com/"
REDHAT_BUGZILLA_PRODUCT="Fedora"
REDHAT_BUGZILLA_PRODUCT_VERSION=32
REDHAT_SUPPORT_PRODUCT="Fedora"
REDHAT_SUPPORT_PRODUCT_VERSION=32
PRIVACY_POLICY_URL="https://fedoraproject.org/wiki/Legal:PrivacyPolicy"
Fedora release 32 (Thirty Two)
Fedora release 32 (Thirty Two)
```

**Output of `uname -a`:**

```
Linux edison 5.9.14-100.fc32.x86_64 #1 SMP Fri Dec 11 14:30:38 UTC 2020 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

**Output of `cat /etc/containers/storage.conf`:**

```
[storage]
driver = "overlay"
runroot = "/var/run/containers/storage"
graphroot = "/var/lib/containers/storage"
[storage.options]
additionalimagestores = [
]
[storage.options.overlay]
mountopt = "nodev,metacopy=on"
[storage.options.thinpool]
```

comments.body:
Buildah and all container engines are just running commands within a container, it knows nothing of these commands and if the commands fail, they fail, and there is nothing the container engine can do about it.

The chfn command is failing when it is executed 

The issue is the lack of CAP_AUDIT_WRITE capabiltiies.  Adding that cap to the container will make this work.
```
# buildah bud --cap-add audit_write /tmp/
STEP 1: FROM adoptopenjdk:11.0.7_10-jdk-hotspot
STEP 2: RUN addgroup --gecos '' foo   && adduser --disabled-password --gecos '' --ingroup foo foo
Adding group `foo' (GID 1000) ...
Done.
Adding user `foo' ...
Adding new user `foo' (1000) with group `foo' ...
Creating home directory `/home/foo' ...
Copying files from `/etc/skel' ...
STEP 3: COMMIT
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 88cc1a200eb9 skipped: already exists  
Copying blob bf509d6bc5ec skipped: already exists  
Copying blob 2af0e1f1e531 skipped: already exists  
Copying blob 4b07a7bca5b7 skipped: already exists  
Copying blob 19503f7a9eec skipped: already exists  
Copying blob ac5b246cca90 skipped: already exists  
Copying blob 893a424494dc done  
Copying config 2fa6b6d64a done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
--> 2fa6b6d64ac
2fa6b6d64acbbc5a752c715931bfb66f77e5219f1c3316f16b748af9bccfde01
```
@rhatdan But why is it that building the same container rootless works without any issue? It only presents an error when running as root.
